rationale: Attacked the three central claims. R5 holds: normalizeImpactedDomains (ownership_resolve.go:72) matches the claimed severity exactly and the call is identical to plan.go:142, so nothing tolerated today is newly rejected — all merged specs and the harness fixture pass. R7b's go-list assertion satisfies the spec's own falsification. Two attacks landed. (1) R6's regex requires ']' immediately after the 4 digits, but ~10 merged specs (085–094) write anchored touchpoint bullets as [ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md](...) inside ## ADR Touchpoints — that in-repo convention is invisible to the check, so an anchored link to a nonexistent ADR in that shape passes spec-approve, triggering R6's falsification clause. Widening is empirically parity-safe: every anchored ID across all specs resolves in the store. (2) R7a's contract test pins only the pure renderers; the RunE exit-code wiring is pinned by two rows any re-derived threshold check also satisfies — a second decision copy there survives every planned gate and exits 0 on a stale-SHA Block. Tally's Warn exit is also unspecified.
concrete_changes_required: Bead 2, step 2 + TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary: widen the extraction regex from \[(ADR-\d{4})\]\([^)]+\) to \[(ADR-\d{4})[^\]]*\]\([^)]+\) so filename-form anchored touchpoint bullets (e.g. [ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md](../../adr/ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md), the convention in specs 085–094: .mindspec/specs/086-doc-sync/spec.md:142, 087:189, 090:81–94, 094:225) are resolved by ID; add a filename-form pair to the test (existing ADR passes, nonexistent fails). Parity-safe today: comm of all anchored IDs in .mindspec/specs/*/spec.md against .mindspec/adr/ is empty.

Bead 4: close the adapter-layer decision hole R7a leaves. TestPanelVerbs_DecisionIsPanelGateDecision pins renderPanelVerify/renderPanelTally, but the tally RunE's exit-code/guard mapping is exercised only on a passing and a sub-threshold row (plus the manual e2e's sub-threshold row) — a RunE that re-derives Allow/Block from res (approves >= threshold) passes every planned gate yet exits 0 on stale-SHA (the lola-f4a8 class), round-mismatch, and hard_block Blocks. Add Block-for-staleness and hard_block rows to TestPanelTally_ExitCodeTracksDecision (via the GateIO/rev-parse seam), or route the RunE through a single decision-to-exit helper asserted over the same GateFacts table as the contract test; additionally assert the rendered PASS/BLOCK token is derived from d.Action, not recomputed.

Bead 4, step 3: pin `panel tally`'s exit behavior on d.Action == panel.Warn (abandoned panel, missing ref, transient git error — gate.go legs 3/5/5b). The step's prose is if-Allow/else-Block shaped, and treating Warn as Block would halt orchestration on abandoned panels with a bogus recovery line. Specify Warn → exit 0 with the advisory message printed (parity with internal/complete's non-blocking Warn handling, panel_advisory.go:204–212) and add a Warn row to TestPanelTally_ExitCodeTracksDecision.

findings.finding: The R6 extraction regex \[(ADR-\d{4})\]\([^)]+\) only matches bracket text that is exactly the bare ID. The repo's dominant historical touchpoint convention (specs 085–094) uses the full filename as bracket text — e.g. .mindspec/specs/086-doc-sync/spec.md:142 `- [ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md](../../adr/ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md)` — inside ## ADR Touchpoints. These anchored bullets are invisible to the check, so a future spec following that convention with a typo'd or deleted ADR sails through spec-approve: exactly the incident class R6 exists to catch, and the spec's falsification clause ('an anchored touchpoint bullet linking a nonexistent ADR passes spec approve') fires. Not 'stricter-than' risk but 'laxer-than-claimed': the check is toothless on a third of real specs. Widening the regex is empirically parity-safe — every anchored ADR ID in every existing spec resolves in the current store (verified by comm against .mindspec/adr/), so no spec validated today newly fails.

Half unfakeable. The contract test pins the pure renderers across Allow/Block/Warn, so a decision copy inside renderPanelVerify/renderPanelTally cannot survive. But the plan's claim 'relocating any decision branch into a CLI adapter breaks the test' over-reaches: the decision-consuming wiring (tally's exit code + guard.NewFailure, verify's printed line) lives in the cobra RunE outside the renderers. TestPanelTally_ExitCodeTracksDecision covers two rows (pass, sub-threshold Block) on which PanelGateDecision and a naive re-derived threshold check agree; the manual e2e adds only another sub-threshold row. An implementer can therefore ship a RunE that recomputes Allow/Block from res.Verdicts, pass every planned verification, and false-exit-0 on stale-SHA (gate.go:209, the lola-f4a8 pin), round-mismatch, and REJECT/hard_block Blocks — violating R3's falsification ('exit code disagrees with PanelGateDecision's Action') undetected. Bounded blast radius (mindspec complete still enforces), but it is precisely the drift class the spec exists to kill.

PanelGateDecision has a third action: Warn (abandoned panel, missing ref after merge, transient git error — gate.go legs 3/5/5b). The plan pins exit 0 on Allow and guard failure on Block but never says what tally does on Warn; the natural if/else implementation treats Warn as Block, making tally exit non-zero on an abandoned panel with a misleading re-panel recovery line, which downstream skill prose will read as a halt. internal/complete treats Warn as advisory-and-proceed (panel_advisory.go:204–212). The Warn row in the R7a contract table pins only the rendered Action, not the exit mapping.

findings.claim_attacked: R7b config-free leaf — go list -deps assertion
findings.finding: The assertion matches the spec's own falsification exactly (only internal/config is pinned), and the adjacent `go build ./...` closes the go-list-fails-silently false-green. Residual gap: the plan's stated invariant is 'import-clean of internal/config (and of git)', but git-freeness is never asserted — a Create that imported internal/executor or shelled out via os/exec would pass every listed gate. Cheap hardening (non-blocking, since the spec pins only config): extend the grep to `internal/(config|executor)`. Test-only imports are outside go list -deps but harmless to the leaf invariant.

findings.claim_attacked: R5 identical-severity claim (attack failed — claim held)
findings.finding: Verified against the real resolver: normalizeImpactedDomains (ownership_resolve.go:72) errors only on path-like zero-owner and path-like multi-owner entries; bare names (Rules 1–2) are kept verbatim with no error, and Bead 2's call (nil exec, "" ownerRef) is byte-identical to plan.go:142. Since plan-approve already runs this exact call over the spec's own Impacted Domains, any spec that passes plan-approve today passes the new spec-approve check by construction. The single 'must pass ValidateSpec' fixture outside the validate package (internal/harness/scenario_spec_lifecycle.go:143) uses a bare-name domain and a link-free touchpoints section, so it survives Bead 2; no doctor/CI surface sweep-validates old specs.
